fetch_status: WebFetch 403; WebSearch fallback returned excerpts from 'The Gentle Singularity' (June 2025) and 'Reflections' (Jan 2025) — primary corpus for atom extraction.

atoms.text: The singularity happens bit by bit; the merge is slow. Exponential technological progress always looks vertical looking forward and flat going backwards, but it's one smooth curve.
2025 marks the arrival of agents that do real cognitive work; writing computer code is permanently transformed. The cognitive labor share of agents in the economy steps up here.
2026 likely brings systems that figure out novel insights; 2027 likely brings robots that act in the real world. The timeline locks cognition before embodiment.
The ability for one person to get much more done in 2030 than they could in 2020 is the visible signature of the gentle singularity; per-capita productivity is the index variable.

OpenAI is now confident it knows how to build AGI as traditionally understood; the aim now extends to superintelligence proper, with superintelligent tools as a force-multiplier on scientific discovery.
In 2025 the first AI agents materially change company output by 'joining the workforce'; the threshold between tool and worker is crossed in a verifiable economic signal.
Safety must be solved while access to superintelligence is widely distributed; uneven distribution is itself a safety failure mode because concentration produces brittle decision regimes.

From a relativistic perspective, observers inside the curve cannot feel the curvature; the gentle subjective experience of an objectively radical transition is an information-theoretic property of being embedded in the dynamics.
AI that can modify and improve its own code has begun to appear in 2025; the recursive self-improvement step that singulatarianism predicted is now a present-tense phenomenon, not a future scenario.
OpenAI's first decade taught that being wrong about strange things publicly is a survival skill; the company is OK with returning to that state when superintelligence sounds science-fictional.

Cognition arrives before embodiment in the gentle curve: agents (2025) -> novel insights (2026) -> robots (2027). The order isn't accidental — verifier-density for cognitive tasks vastly exceeds verifier-density for physical tasks.

Massively accelerated scientific discovery via superintelligent tools is the primary economic argument for pursuing superintelligence; abundance and prosperity scale with discovery rate, not with model size per se.
